prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A scientist's hands at a wooden desk, sketching with a pencil on white paper — drawing a clean, minimal diagram of an atom (a small central nucleus with three concentric electron orbits). On the desk beside the paper: an open notebook, scattered measurement tools, a half-drunk cup of chai. Through a tall window in the background, the night sky is visible — real swirling galaxies, nebulae, complex cosmic detail rendered in soft cool blues. The visual contrast between the simple sketched model and the unimaginable complexity of the actual universe is the emotional point. Warm desk lamp light on the paper, cool starlight through the window. Painterly illustration style with cinematic depth of field. Dark background. No text, no labels.
alt: A scientist's hands sketching a simplified diagram of an atom while real galaxies are visible through a window — choosing what to leave out is part of seeing

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A young Indian teenager in school uniform sits at a kitchen table at home, smartphone in hand, mid-thought. The phone screen is faintly visible — showing what looks like a forwarded message with a dramatic claim. The student's expression is thoughtful, neither anxious nor dismissive — the expression of someone *deciding what to think*. Around them on the table: a glass of water, a half-eaten apple, an open notebook. Through the window in the background, soft daylight, and the faint silhouettes of a microscope and an old astronomical instrument on a shelf — symbols of careful inquiry. The visual point: between the rush of forwarded information and the patience of actual investigation, a young person is choosing the second. Warm domestic light, painterly illustration style, calm and contemplative. Dark muted background. No text overlay, no labels.
alt: A young Indian student pauses with their phone in hand, the screen showing a viral message, while behind them, faintly, are the silhouettes of scientific instruments — discernment between true and false

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A forest ashram clearing at the edge of dawn in ancient India. In the foreground, a Rishi — calm, absorbed, unhurried — sits cross-legged on stone, tracing mathematical calculations onto a flat surface with a finger. Beside him: a rolled palm-leaf manuscript, a copper sighting instrument pointed at the horizon, and a precise geometric yantra drawn in the earth. In the middle distance, other figures work quietly — one writes by lamplight, one holds an astrolabe to the fading stars. The sky above is transitioning from star-filled deep indigo to the first pale gold of sunrise. The image conveys: sustained, rigorous observation as a daily practice. Deep indigo and dawn-gold palette, lush but not fantastical, atmospheric. Painterly epic Indian art style. Dark background. No text, no labels.
alt: A Rishi sits in a forest ashram at dawn tracing astronomical calculations by hand, surrounded by manuscripts, a copper instrument, and a geometric yantra drawn in the earth — direct inquiry in ancient India

prompt: A clean, dramatic square illustration representing ancient Indian astronomy. A large geometric astronomical sundial instrument (like those at Jantar Mantar) shown in warm amber afternoon light against a deep indigo sky. Mathematical notations and celestial arc diagrams etched into the instrument. Stars beginning to appear at dusk. Style: precise scientific illustration, warm and awe-inspiring. Square format. No text overlay. No people.

An elegant square mathematical illustration representing the concept of zero as both philosophical breakthrough and mathematical tool. A perfect circle (zero) drawn in gold ink on aged parchment, surrounded by ancient Sanskrit mathematical notation and number sequences. Warm candlelight illuminates the manuscript from the side, casting gentle shadows. Style: historical mathematical manuscript meets editorial illustration. Warm amber and gold tones on deep parchment. Square format. No text overlay.

A square illustration representing ancient Indian medicine and anatomical knowledge. Precise line drawings of the human circulatory and nervous systems, in the style of ancient Indian medical manuscripts — elegant, detailed, systematic. The diagrams are drawn on aged palm-leaf or parchment in warm sepia and gold ink. Style: ancient medical illustration meets modern scientific infographic. Warm tones. Square format. No text overlay.
alt: चरक और सुश्रुत (Charaka & Sushruta) · c. 600–400 BCE · Medicine

prompt: An abstract square scientific illustration representing atomic theory. A single luminous particle (Parmanu) at the center, with concentric energy rings emanating outward — like a Bohr model but rendered in ancient Indian geometric style. Tiny particles in amber and gold swirl outward to form larger matter. Deep space indigo background with Sanskrit script fragments barely visible. Style: scientific visualization meets ancient geometric art. Square format. No text overlay.
alt: आचार्य कणाद (Acharya Kanad) · c. 600 BCE · Atomic Theory

prompt: A square illustration representing binary mathematics discovered through Sanskrit poetry. A grid of binary patterns (0s and 1s arranged as laghu-guru rhythmic symbols) flows downward and transforms into Pascal's triangle at the bottom. The patterns are rendered in cool silver-white on deep indigo, suggesting both ancient script and modern computation. Style: mathematical visualization with a poetic quality — precision and beauty together. Square format. No text overlay.

A precise, clean square illustration representing the science of attention and consciousness. Concentric rings shown as a mandala-like diagram but explicitly scientific in character — not religious. The rings move from gross thought at the outer edge to pure awareness at the center. Neural connectivity lines are subtly visible within the structure, suggesting the meeting of ancient inner science and modern neuroscience. Style: scientific diagram meets meditative geometry. Cool indigo, silver, and deep teal tones. Square format. No text overlay.
alt: पतञ्जलि (Patanjali) · c. 200 BCE · Science of Mind
